title: glochidion submolle
intro: Glochidion submolle is a plant species in the family Phyllanthaceae, commonly known as a type of Euphorbiaceae and also referred to as the "Sapindus tree" however, it is in fact not related to the Sapindus tree, but is in fact a member of the Euphorbiaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Glochidion submolle include its ability to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to protect against certain diseases. Herbal preparations of Glochidion submolle include infusions and decoctions made from the leaves, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
